There's no dashboard of what worktrees exist, what's running, or a clean switcher. `git worktree list` is the only "overview." 2. **The `WorktreeCreate` hook is all-or-nothing.** It replaces *all* git logic *and* disables `.worktreeinclude`, so you can't say "native, but also run `pnpm i` after." You either get the simple path or you reimplement the whole thing in one bash blob. 3. **Rigid `worktree-<name>` branch naming** unless you write that hook. 4. **Cleanup leaks** — `-p`/non-interactive runs don't auto-clean, so you're left running `git worktree remove` by hand. 5. **Same disk + dependency duplication** as any worktree approach (install per tree, duplicate dev servers/DBs). {"command":"python3 - <<'PY'
import json,re, pathlib
root=pathlib.Path('.')
cat=json.load(open('Strive/Application/Main/Localizable.xcstrings'))['strings']
keys=set(cat.keys())
files=[p for p in root.rglob('*.swift') if not any(part in {'StriveTests','Strive DesktopTests'} for part in p.parts)]
# Best-effort single-line Swift string literal extractor.
str_re=re.compile(r'"(?:\\.|[^"\\])*"')
word_re=re.compile(r'[A-Za-z][A-Za-z ]{2,}')
skip_substrings=['Image(systemName:', 'Color("', 'UIFont', 'NSFont', 'UserDefaults', 'Bundle.main', 'AnalyticsManager', '.capture(', 'Log.', 'print(', 'fatalError(', 'dateFormat', 'URL(string:', '@AppStorage', 'forKey:', 'rawValue', 'appGroup', 'group.org', 'http://', 'https://', 'strive://', '#Preview', 'PreviewProvider', 'IntentDescription(', '@Parameter', 'kind: String', 'font:', 'identifier', 'accessibilityIdentifier']
skip_literals=set(['', ' ', '\n', '*', 'none', 'true', 'false'])
rows=[]
for p in files:
    try: lines=p.read_text(errors='ignore').splitlines()
    except Exception: continue
    for i,line in enumerate(lines,1):
        for m in str_re.finditer(line):
            lit=m.group()[1:-1]
            if lit in keys: continue
            if lit in skip_literals: continue
            if not word_re.search(lit): continue
            if any(s in line for s in skip_substrings): continue
            # Skip likely identifiers/SF symbols/lowercase slugs/date fmt/course ids.
            if re.fullmatch(r'[a-z0-9_./:-]+', lit): continue
            if re.fullmatch(r'[a-z]+(\.[a-z0-9]+)+', lit): continue
            if re.fullmatch(r'[A-Z0-9_]+', lit): continue
            # Keep contexts that are plausibly user-facing or used to feed UI.
            keep=False
            ui_markers=['Text(', 'Button(', 'Label(', 'TextField(', 'SecureField(', 'Picker(', 'Toggle(', 'Section(', 'ProgressView(', '.alert(', '.confirmationDialog(', '.navigationTitle(', '.accessibilityLabel(', '.accessibilityHint(', '.accessibilityValue(', '.help(', '.description(', '.configurationDisplayName(', 'title:', 'subtitle:', 'message:', 'prompt:', 'errorMessage', 'return ', '= "', ': "']
            if any(k in line for k in ui_markers): keep=True
            # Also keep string returns/assignments in Presentation/Domain legal/tip files.
            if any(part in {'Presentation','Widgets'} for part in p.parts): keep=True
            if not keep: continue
            rows.append((str(p),i,lit,line.strip()))

for p,i,lit,line in rows:
    print(f'{p}:{i}\t{lit}\t{line}')
print(f'\nTOTAL {len(rows)}', file __import__('sys').stderr)
PY"}

{"command":"python3 - <<'PY'
import json,re,pathlib,collections
keys=set(json.load(open('Strive/Application/Main/Localizable.xcstrings'))['strings'].keys())
patterns=[
('StandardButton', r'StandardButton\(title: "([^"]+)"'),
('plain title arg', r'\btitle: "([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
('plain description arg', r'\bdescription: "([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
('error assignment', r'\berror(?:Message)?\s*=\s*"([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
('notification title/body', r'content\.(?:title|body)\s*=\s*"([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
('return String', r'return "([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
('event notes', r'event\.set\(notes: "([^"]*[A-Za-z][^"]*)"'),
]
for name,pat in patterns:
    rx=re.compile(pat)
    print('\n##',name)
    count=0
    for p in pathlib.Path('.').rglob('*.swift'):
        if any(part in {'StriveTests','Strive DesktopTests'} for part in p.parts): continue
        text=p.read_text(errors='ignore').splitlines()
        for i,line in enumerate(text,1):
            for m in rx.finditer(line):
                s=m.group(1)
                if s in keys: continue
                if any(x in line for x in ['Log.', 'AnalyticsManager', '#Preview', '@available', 'request.setValue', 'URL(string:', 'Image(systemName:']): continue
                print(f'{p}:{i}: {s}')
                count+=1
    print('count',count)
PY"}
